title: Deformation resistant wound therapy apparatus and related methods of use
abstract: A wound therapy apparatus is disclosed herein that may include a wound interface sealingly securable to the skin surface around a wound bed to enclose the wound bed within a fluid-tight enclosed space. Fluid may be communicated with the enclosed space in order to vary the pressure p0 within the enclosed space periodically over the pressure range pmin â¤ p0 â¤ pmax. The wound interface may be sufficiently deformation resistant to allow distention of at least a portion of the wound bed into the enclosed space when pressure p0 within the enclosed space is less than ambient pressure pamb. The wound therapy apparatus may include a pad that communicates with the distended wound bed to absorb exudate emanating from the distended wound bed. Related methods of use of the wound therapy apparatus are also disclosed herein.
claims:
Claims The invention claimed is : 1. An apparatus, comprising: a wound interface that is deformation resistant and sealingly securable to the skin surface to form an enclosed space that is fluid-tight and encloses a wound boundary of a wound bed; a port disposed about the wound interface in fluid communication with the enclosed space for withdrawal of fluid from the enclosed space to produce pressure po less than ambient pressure p am b within the enclosed space; and a distended state that comprises at least a portion of the wound bed distended through an enclosed space entry into the enclosed space by pressure po less than ambient pressure p am b when the wound interface is sealingly secured to the skin surface. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a pad receivable within the enclosed space to absorb exudate from the wound bed by intermittent engagement with the wound bed, the pad received within the enclosed space fluidly communicates at least intermittently with the port for withdrawal of the exudate from the pad through the port. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the pad comprises a material selected from polyvinyl alcohol, polyurethane, aliphatic polyamide fiber, semi-aromatic polyamide fiber, and polyester fiber. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a pad receivable within the enclosed space to absorb exudate from the wound bed by varying degree of engagement with the wound bed, the pad fluidly communicates at least intermittently with the port for withdrawal of the exudate from the pad through the port. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, further comprising: a periodic variation of the pressure po within the pressure range p mm â¤ poâ¤ p ma x when the wound interface is sealingly secured to the skin surface, the periodic variation of the 36 

 pressure po alters the wound bed between the distended state and a baseline state to massage the wound bed. 6. A wound therapy apparatus, comprising: a wound interface sealingly securable to the skin surface around a wound bed to enclose a wound boundary of a wound bed within an enclosed space that is fluid-tight, the wound interface is sufficiently deformation resistant to distend at least a portion of the wound bed into the enclosed space when pressure po within the enclosed space is less than ambient pressure pamb; a port disposed about the wound interface to communicate fluid with the enclosed space in order to vary the pressure po within the enclosed space periodically over the pressure range p mm â¤ poâ¤ p ma x where p mm â¤ pamb; and a pad receivable within the enclosed space to absorb exudate emanating from the wound bed, the variation of the pressure po distends the wound bed into communication with the pad and releases the wound bed from communication with the pad. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein pressure p ma x is approximately equal to ambient pressure pamb. 8. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the port is in at least intermittent fluid communication with the pad for withdrawal of exudate from the pad. 9. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the pad comprises knitted fibers with hydrophobic polyester fiber predominant on a surface and hydroplnlic nylon fibers predominantly on an interior and at point of contact with evacuation port, to serve as a conduit for fluid transfer. 10. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the pad comprises a material selected from polyvinyl alcohol, polyurethane, aliphatic polyamide fiber, semi-aromatic polyamide fiber, and polyester fiber. 11. The apparatus of claim 6, further comprising: an interstice formed by the wound interface, the pad disposed within the interstice; and 37 

 one or more passages between the enclosed space and the interstice to communicate exudate from the wound bed to the pad. 12. The apparatus of claim 6, the pad is replaceably receivable by the wound interface. 13. The apparatus of claim 6, the wound interface further comprising: a cover positionable between a sealing engagement position and an open engagement position that allows direct intervention into the enclosed space. 14. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the wound bed is viewable through a transparent portion of the wound interface. 15. The apparatus of claim 6, the wound interface further comprising: a cushion that forms a closed annular circumference, the cushion comprising an inflatable sac or closed-cell foam, and the cushion adhesively sealingly secured to the skin surface to sealingly secure the wound interface to the skin surface. 16. The apparatus of claim 6, the wound interface further comprising: an apron that extends forth to form a closed perimeter for conformably sealing securement of the wound interface to the skin surface. 17. The apparatus of claim 6, the wound interface further comprising: a flange disposed about an outer surface of the wound interface at a distal end to sealingly conformably secure the wound interface to the skin surface, the flange operable to distribute over the skin surface forces from pressure po within the enclosed space. 18. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the wound interface sealingly securable to an ovoid or elongated shaped region of skin surface around the wound boundary of the wound bed. The apparatus of claim 6, comprising: 38 

 capillaries proximate the wound bed that have blood flow increased above baseline with wound bed in distended state. 20. The apparatus of claim 6, comprising: capillaries proximate the wound bed that alternate between a dilated state and a non- dilated state in correspondence as the wound bed alternates between distended state and baseline state by periodic variation of the pressure po within the enclosed space over the pressure range p mm â¤ poâ¤ p ma x. 21. A wound therapy apparatus, comprising: a wound interface sealingly secured to the skin surface around a wound bed to encloses a wound boundary of a wound bed within an enclosed space that is fluid-tight, the wound interface is sufficiently deformation resistant to distend at least a portion of the wound bed into the enclosed space when pressure po within the enclosed space is less than ambient pressure pamb; a port disposed about the wound interface to communicate fluid with the enclosed space in order to vary the pressure po within the enclosed space periodically over the pressure range p mm â¤ po â¤ p ma x where p mm â¤ p am b; and a pad received within the enclosed space to absorb exudate emanating from the wound bed, the variation of the pressure po distends the wound bed into communication with the pad and releases the wound bed from communication with the pad. 22. The wound therapy apparatus of claim 21 , the pressure po within the enclosed space is varied periodically over the pressure range pmm < po < p max over less than about 6 minutes. 23. The wound therapy apparatus of claim 21 , the pressure pmax is generally greater than or equal to the ambient pressure pamb. 39
